representative_artifacts.observation: The animated GIF visibly carries the black-and-ivory checker pattern and coral registration tile around the sphere; their changing screen orientation makes rotation readily noticeable while the sphere advances and later reverses.
The MP4 visibly carries the checker pattern and coral registration tile throughout the motion, with no missing-texture fallback or loading artifact observed.

The five-time panel shows the checker quadrants and coral registration tile in materially different orientations, making the sphere's rotation visible alongside its translational advance and reversal.
At step 0 the checker texture and coral registration tile are crisp and legible on the sphere.
At step 1 the checker boundary has visibly rotated relative to step 0, while the coral tile has moved out of the same view.
At step 2 the coral tile has rotated back into view and the checker boundaries differ visibly from steps 0 and 1.
